title: Smoky Crispy Venezuelan Pork Stew
description: A hearty, smoky pork stew with crispy shoulder steaks and a tangy, savory broth that soothes hangovers and delivers comforting Venezuelan flavor.
instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp of olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat and sear the pork steaks until golden brown on both sides, about 3–4 minutes per side. Remove the steaks and set aside.
Add the remaining 1 Tbsp of oil, diced onion, minced garlic, and sliced bell pepper to the pot; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the smoked paprika, cumin, and smoked sea salt; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the diced tomatoes with their juice and the vegetable broth; add the bay leaf. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and let simmer for 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Return the seared pork steaks to the pot, nestling them in the broth. Cover and simmer gently for 30–35 minutes, until the pork is tender and the broth has thickened slightly.
Discard the bay leaf and check seasoning, adding more salt or pepper if desired.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime over the top for brightness.
Feel free to accompany with crusty bread or rice for a complete meal.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
